title: Restore harness-status skill synchronization
description: Treat .claude/skills/harness-status/SKILL.md as read-only canonical input for this task. Own .agents/skills/harness-status/SKILL.md, skills/harness-status/SKILL.md, and .harness/tests/test_codex_integration.py. Resolve the observed divergence by making the reusable Codex/plugin copies byte-identical to the Claude skill and extend the existing sync test to compare all three. Do not edit any .claude file. Acceptance command: python3 -m unittest discover -s .harness/tests -p test_codex_integration.py -v. The test output must report a nonzero test count and fail on future byte divergence.

notes.note: DECISION: Treat .claude/skills/harness-status/SKILL.md as read-only canonical bytes; synchronize both reusable copies and extend the Codex integration test to assert all three surfaces remain identical.

DECISION: Coordinated with T-312 through the parent dispatcher: while holding test_codex_integration.py, updated the exact Codex agent profile expectation from five to six and added context_scout so the concurrent parity port will not regress the full suite.

DEVIATION: The acceptance test's pre-existing blackboard smoke assertion assumed an empty Codex frontier, but this follow-up epic intentionally has open Codex work. Made it state-robust: either a legal dispatch or the no-claimable response proves engine routing, while preserving the original assertions for each branch. Kept pre-existing whitespace-tolerant sync for unrelated skills; harness-status alone now uses byte equality across Claude, project, and plugin.
RESULT: Project Codex harness-status skill synchronized byte-for-byte from read-only Claude canonical input; SHA-256 c6d697ba94bae8127545bd550c5ea2ffaeef4051dcab457738bdae34ea4bff7f.
RESULT: Plugin harness-status skill synchronized to the same canonical bytes.
RESULT: Regression test now asserts byte equality for Claude/project/plugin harness-status surfaces; coordinated exact six-agent expectation with T-312.

HANDOFF -> verifier: Replay: python3 -m unittest discover -s .harness/tests -p test_codex_integration.py -v => 6 tests, OK (2 TOML skips under Python 3.9); python3.12 same command => 6 tests, all OK. shasum -a 256 .claude/skills/harness-status/SKILL.md .agents/skills/harness-status/SKILL.md skills/harness-status/SKILL.md => identical c6d697ba94bae8127545bd550c5ea2ffaeef4051dcab457738bdae34ea4bff7f. git diff --check on owned files => clean. Claude canonical was read-only; no .claude file changed. Verify the byte-diff assertion fails if either downstream copy is perturbed, then restore it without editing Claude.
VERIFICATION START: replay Python 3.9 and 3.12 suites, exact byte hashes, six-agent expectation, scoped diff check, and non-mutating perturbation simulation.

VERIFIED: exact acceptance replay produced 6 tests on Python 3.9 (4 pass, 2 expected TOML skips) and 6/6 pass on Python 3.12. Claude canonical, project skill and plugin skill have identical SHA-256 c6d697ba94bae8127545bd550c5ea2ffaeef4051dcab457738bdae34ea4bff7f. Scoped diff check clean. Independent non-mutating Path.read_bytes perturbation of plugin copy made the targeted sync test fail exactly once, proving byte drift detection. Six-profile assertion includes context_scout. Claude canonical was read only.
